title: Work Event
description: The single typed record the AI-Memory event-log writer appends (FR-1) and the generated daily + per-project views render from (FR-2). One unit of logged work = exactly one of these records. The human daily-note and per-project (PJL) views are generatable from a stream of these events; no second hand-authored copy is kept.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the unit of work occurred (event time, not log time). Example: 2026-06-13T14:32:00-06:00.

project.description: The project this event belongs to. Drives per-project view grouping. Use the canonical project name (matches 02_Projects/<project>/), or a standalone label for cross-cutting work.

type.description: The kind of event. Controls how the renderer groups/labels the entry.

summary.description: One-line human-readable outcome statement. The headline rendered in the daily and per-project views. Name the distinct outcome (what shipped / what was fixed / what was decided).

details.description: Optional longer-form body for the agent-readable layer (the PJL-equivalent view). Specifics a future agent needs to resume.

paths.description: Files, directories, or other filesystem paths touched by this unit of work.

decisions.description: Decisions made during this unit of work and (ideally) why, so future agents do not re-litigate them.

rationale.description: Why it was decided this way.

ref.description: Optional wikilink or identifier for the authority (ADR, SPC, lesson).

tried_and_failed.description: Approaches attempted that did not work, so future agents do not repeat the failure.

artifacts.description: Wikilinks to artifacts created or updated by this unit of work (specs, plans, PICs, IRs, ADRs, reports). Format: [[Doc Name]].

items.description: An Obsidian wikilink, e.g. [[RE - AI-Memory Vault P1 Baseline]].

provenance.description: FR-3. Origin metadata stamped on every event. Lets retrieval and the stale-premise detector attribute a fact to its source session and authoring agent.

origin_session.description: Identifier of the session that produced this event (session id, transcript path, or a stable session label).

author_agent.description: The agent or actor that authored this event (e.g. 'schemas-worker', 'orchestrator', 'operator', or a model/role label).
